title: I spent two weeks with the Galaxy S26 and Galaxy S26 Plus — and only one is worth buying
summary: Don't buy the wrong Samsung phone this year.
body: Whenever new Galaxy S phones are released, the conclusion for them is usually the same: Get the baseline model if you want a compact phone, get the Plus if you want a bigger screen without spending too much, and get the Ultra if you want Samsung’s absolute best. That thinking holds true for the Galaxy S26 Ultra , but what about Samsung’s other two phones? Are the Galaxy S26 () and Galaxy S26 Plus () still safe recommendations for Samsung fans who need an upgrade this year?

title: I’ve completely switched to Qi2 power banks, and I can’t go back
summary: The convenience of charging in my pocket without even looking has transformed how I use my power banks.
body: Credit: Rita El Khoury / Android Authority When I realized that my brand new Pixel 10 Pro XL doesn’t work well with my existing Qi power banks , I went looking for new Qi2 options and assumed I would use them the same way I used my previous portable batteries, wireless or not. Plug in or magnetically attach when my phone’s battery is running low on a busy day, give it an hour or so, and manage to make it to the end of the day without the phone dying. But over the last few months, something slowly shifted in the way I’ve been using the few Qi2 power banks I own. Of course, they charge my phone — I haven’t figured out a magical trick to use them in a different way — but it’s the when and how that have changed for me.

title: The Nothing Phone 4a Pro brings the vibes and great value for under $500
summary: Nothing's first A series phone to officially hit the US is a flawed, but fancy mid-ranger.
body: My first experience with a Nothing smartphone was the Nothing Phone 3 . For what was intended to be the company’s first “true flagship,” it didn’t exactly blow me away, but I did fall in love with its design. It stood out; it was different. Now, the newly released Nothing Phone 4a Pro () is officially bringing Nothing’s unique style to the mid-range in the US for the first time. Can it deliver? Let’s find out.

title: 5 things I set up on every Android phone to boost my productivity
summary: Always the same, regardless of the device.
body: Credit: Andy Walker / Android Authority Story time: The plan is to check my email, and all of a sudden, I’m on YouTube watching a Galaxy S26 review , jumping between various apps based on the plethora of notifications I received, and checking the results of the last F1 race — go Kimi. A task that should take me two minutes turns into an hour of mindless online activity, and the worst part is that I forgot to check my email in the process. A phone is a wonderful tool, but it can also be a distracting one. And since I have a habit of picking it up mindlessly all too often, I try to reduce that as much as possible by setting up five things on every Android device I use. While these don’t prevent my trips down various rabbit holes 100% of the time, they do minimize them and save me a lot of time and mental energy in the process that I can use for other activities, boosting my overall productivity.

title: Netflix may have to pay back years of price hikes to some subscribers — but probably not you
summary: The court ruling only applies in Italy, and lands just days after Netflix pushed through another round increases in the US.
body: Credit: Edgar Cervantes / Android Authority TL;DR An Italian court has ruled that several of Netflix’s past price hikes were unlawful. Affected subscribers in Italy may be entitled to refunds and reduced subscription prices. The ruling comes just after Netflix raised prices again in the US, but Netflix is appealing. While Netflix subscribers in the US are still reeling from yet another round of price hikes , the streaming service has just been told to give some of its users the exact opposite treatment. A court in Rome has ruled that several of its past price increases in Italy were unlawful, potentially entitling affected subscribers in the country to refunds and lower current prices. According to Ars Technica , the case was brought by Italian consumer group Movimento Consumatori, which argued that Netflix had raised prices in ways that violated the country’s Consumer Code. The issue was that Netflix’s contracts reportedly didn’t properly explain in advance why those prices or other terms might change in the future. In other words, the platform hadn’t adequately justified the price increases before imposing them — a feeling you might share wherever you live.

title: More evidence for Samsung’s secret new Galaxy Buds just surfaced
summary: The "Galaxy Buds Able" is still a mystery, but its battery just got certified.
body: Credit: Paul Jones / Android Authority TL;DR The battery unit for the Galaxy Buds Able was spotted on the Bureau of Indian Standards. This battery unit appears in the database with the model number EB-BU600AAY. Through an APK teardown earlier this month, we learned that Samsung appeared to be working on a new entry to the Galaxy Buds line. The text strings suggested that this entry may be called the “Galaxy Buds Able” and that it may carry the model number SM-U600. Bringing this device closer to reality, a part for the device has now received certification. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: Catch a 37% discount on this highly rated security camera just months after launch
summary: The Baseus X1 Pro brings dual 3K cameras, solar charging, and local storage, and it’s $100 off right now.
body: Credit: Baseus Outdoor security cameras can get expensive fast once you start looking for the nicer extras. Solar charging, local storage, and smart tracking usually don’t come cheap, so a feature-packed 2026 model already at a hefty discount is always going to catch our eye. Right now, the Baseus X1 Pro is down to $169.97 on Amazon — a full 37% off its $269.99 list price. Baseus X1 Pro Dual-Tracking Solar Security Camera for 169.97 ($100 off)

title: Chrome is testing a new feature for speeding up browsing on even the heaviest pages
summary: Tired of slow page loads? Chrome's new lazy loading capabilities will help.
body: Credit: Andy Walker / Android Authority TL;DR Google is testing lazy loading of video and audio elements for Chrome 148. This feature defers the loading of video and audio resources to speed up page performance and reduce data usage. Lazy loading will roll out for all users on desktop and mobile. Chrome 148 is currently in development and is expected to roll out in mid-April. When the update arrives, it could make your daily browsing a little faster. Google is testing lazy loading for video and audio elements (via PCWorld ) . Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: It’s a good Friday to score a $150 cash discount on the Galaxy Tab S11
summary: Samsung’s flagship tablet starts at $649.99 with this deal, which makes it a lot easier to justify.
body: Credit: C. Scott Brown / Android Authority Samsung’s flagship tablet lineup has a pretty obvious overkill option in the form of the huge Tab S11 Ultra. That’s great for power users, but for many people, the regular Galaxy Tab S11 probably makes more sense , and this new Samsung deal helps that case along nicely. Samsung Galaxy Tab S11 for $649.99 ($150 off)

title: Everybody’s trying to get one of Google and Back Market’s $3 ChromeOS Flex keys
summary: They're currently out of stock.
body: TL;DR Google and Back Market are selling $3 USB keys that come preloaded with ChromeOS Flex. The keys simplify getting ChromeOS up and running on older hardware. These new keys are out of stock as of publication. Last month, Back Market and Google introduced a low-friction new way to get some extra mileage out of older PCs and Macs: ready-made ChromeOS Flex USB keys that make it simple to get Google’s desktop OS up and running on computers that are out of software support or struggling to run heavier operating systems. Those USB keys are now up for sale — and they’re surprisingly cheap. Google announced availability of the new USB-A keys in a blog post. They’re being sold through Back Market for just a few bucks, and come with simple instructions explaining how to use them to boot ChromeOS on a Windows PC or Mac.

title: Trouble with blurry Galaxy S26 Ultra photos? Samsung now has a fix
summary: Getting blurry shots on your Galaxy S26 Ultra? You'll want to update your phone.
body: Credit: Ryan Haines / Android Authority TL;DR Some Galaxy S26 Ultra users have been experiencing blurry photos when shooting at 3x zoom. It appears the problem gets worse when shooting in low light with the flash on. Samsung has released a fix in the April 2026 security patch. Now that the Galaxy S26 Ultra has been out for a few weeks, you’ve probably had some time to play around with the cameras. If you think you noticed some blurriness when shooting at 3x zoom, you’re not imagining things. Owners have been reporting seeing the same issue with their handsets. Fortunately, Samsung is on top of it and already has a fix ready. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: I spent a week with Android 17 Beta 3 on my Pixel, and these 4 features change everything
summary: Android 17 is looking pretty darn good.
body: A little over a week ago, Google began rolling out the latest update to the Android 17 Beta — specifically, Android 17 Beta 3 . Beta 1 and Beta 2 both proved to be pretty light on user-facing changes, and Beta 3 has already shown it has a lot more to offer. I’ve had Android 17 Beta 3 downloaded on one of my Pixels since its release, and after my first week with it, there are four features in particular that I can’t get enough of.

title: Why the $339 Pixel 8a is the only cheap Pixel I’d buy in 2026
summary: If I needed a replacement phone tomorrow, I would buy a refurbished Pixel 8a.
body: Consumer electronics are getting more expensive. From PCs to smartphones to game consoles , AI-induced component shortages have us all paying more for tech, and with global events once again constraining supply chains, the cost of those goods doesn’t seem likely to come down soon. Given the givens, you might be looking to save a buck on your next phone. In that case, I’ve got a recommendation for you: consider picking up a refurbished Pixel 8a . Google’s been selling refurbished Pixel phones for a while now, but the pricing of its secondhand options hasn’t really made a good case for buying a reconditioned phone versus a brand-new one.

title: How much will RAMageddon hike flagship prices? Top phone executive reveals the cost
summary: RAM and memory costs have nearly quadrupled in a year.
body: Credit: Robert Triggs / Android Authority TL;DR Smartphone RAM and storage costs have nearly quadrupled over the past year, with a 12GB + 512GB configuration now costing manufacturers approximately $218 more, according to a top Xiaomi executive. While brands have used existing inventory to maintain lower prices, those supplies are running out, leading to predicted flagship price hikes of $100-$200. RAM prices have kept climbing for much of this year, shocking the supply chain and forcing brands across product categories to pass on the rising costs to consumers or even outright kill some products . While it’s easy to see the skyrocketing prices of components in PCs, it’s much harder for consumers to notice the price increases on phones. If you’ve been wondering just how bad RAMageddon will pinch you on your next phone purchase, we finally have some credible numbers to relate to. Xiaomi Group President Mr. Lu Weibing took to Weibo to inform consumers about the recent memory price increase.

title: Samsung could finally equip this Galaxy A mid-ranger with a more capable selfie camera
summary: Bringing it on par with the newly launched Galaxy A37.
body: Credit: Rushil Agrawal / Android Authority TL;DR Samsung’s upcoming Galaxy A27 has leaked. The report hints at a new 12MP selfie camera with 4K recording capabilities. According to a recent Geekbench listing, the phone could come with a Snapdragon 6 Gen 3. It’s just the beginning of the second quarter of the year, and Samsung has already wrapped up key non-flagship launches for the year. The Galaxy A07 and A17 came last year, and were recently joined by the Galaxy A37 and A57 . And now, the only missing piece of the puzzle may be joining soon, as Samsung prepares to launch the Galaxy A27. The specifications for the Galaxy A27 have recently been leaked by the Dutch technology site GalaxyClub . The leaks hint at possible improvements to the camera over last year’s Galaxy A26 , including a 12MP selfie camera. Although the resolution of this sensor has been reduced from 13MP, GalaxyClub says the new sensor is actually an upgrade, as it captures more light, which, in turn, leads to better sharpness in low-light selfies.

title: ANBERNIC’s screen-flipping handheld finally has a name
summary: We finally know the name of ANBERNIC's mysterious handheld.
body: Credit: Qinki001 TL;DR A leak has provided another look at ANBERNIC’s upcoming handheld. The leak reveals that the device is called the “RG Rotate.” It’s also revealed that the RG Rotate has a 2000mAh battery. Over the last few weeks, we’ve been seeing leaks of a mysterious ANBERNIC handheld with a swiveling display. Just yesterday, we reported that the device showed up in a couple of new videos . Now, the latest leak may have finally revealed the name of this handheld. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: PSA: CMF Watch owners need to switch over to a new app
summary: You'll be able to transfer some of your information, but not all of it.
body: Credit: Kaitlyn Cimino / Android Authority TL;DR The CMF Watch app is getting removed from app stores on April 3, 2026. CMF watches will need to start pairing and connecting to the Nothing X app on July 19, 2026. The migration process will transfer data such as workout records, daily activity, heart rate, active score, fitness goals, and more. Custom watch faces created in the CMF Watch app won’t transfer. It has been almost three years since the CMF Watch app launched on the Google Play Store. Now its time is coming to an end as Nothing plans to sunset the app. If you’re still using the CMF Watch app for your CMF Watch , you’ll wan’t to start migrating over to the Nothing X app as soon as possible. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: We loved this wireless CarPlay and Android Auto adapter, and now it’s finally dropped in price
summary: This adapter won us over in review, but it was always full price until now.
body: Credit: C. Scott Brown / Android Authority If your car still makes you plug in for Android Auto or Apple CarPlay, the AAWireless TWO Plus is built to fix that annoyance. It’s now down to $55.24 on Amazon from its usual $65, marking the first discount we’ve tracked on a device we rated highly since it launched last year. AAWireless TWO Plus for $55.24 ($10 off)

title: Google is fixing the most annoying thing about Chrome’s split view feature
summary: Chrome is making it harder to accidentally activate split view.
body: TL;DR Google is fixing a common frustration with Chrome’s split view feature. The option is being moved below the “Open link in new window” option to prevent accidental clicks. There will not be a master toggle to disable the feature “at this time.” When Chrome 145 rolled out earlier this year, it introduced a highly requested feature to the browser — split view . This feature allows the user to put two tabs side-by-side in a single window for improved multitasking. While the feature works as intended, it has also been the cause of some frustration. Google has heard the complaints and has announced plans to fix the issue. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: Can’t make it to Coachella? YouTube’s upgraded livestream experience has you covered
summary: YouTube is turning your living room into Coachella with 7 live stages and Multiview.
body: Credit: Google TL;DR YouTube has announced it will be livestreaming Coachella. The platform will be livestreaming seven stages simultaneously. You’ll be able to set up a Multiview to watch up to four streams at the same time. About a week from now, it will be one of the best times of the year for avid music fans. Coachella kicks off on April 10, and there will be a variety of artists on stage, from Sabrina Carpenter and Justin Bieber to Karol G and Young Thug. If you can’t attend the show, you’ll still be able to catch all the action from your living room. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: Best Galaxy A56 5G deal yet slashes the price by $115
summary: Samsung’s mid-ranger looks a lot more appealing at its lowest Amazon price to date.
body: Credit: Paul Jones / Android Authority The Samsung Galaxy A56 5G always felt like a phone that needed a better price to really make sense. That price has now arrived, with Amazon dropping the Android phone to $384.99 from $499.99. As for Amazon, this is the lowest price we’ve seen yet for the handset. Samsung Galaxy A56 5G for $384.99 ($115 off)

title: Expecto Pixtronum! Google’s going to Hogwarts for upcoming Pixel themes
summary: Google's preparing a magical new Pixel theme pack for all you wizards.
body: TL;DR As SpongeBob gets ready to sunset, Google’s prepping two new sets of Pixel themes. One is all about Harry Potter, with magic and Hogwarts wallpapers and sound effects to customize your phone. It also looks like Google’s prepping some artistic new options from HERALBONY in Japan. Google introduced Pixel theme packs last year for quickly overhauling your phone’s look, and while we wish we had a little more variety there, we have to admit — they’re pretty great. Depending on who you ask, the current SpongeBob offering may be the best thing to happen to Pixel phones in a while, even if we’re super bummed to only have it around for another month. As we brace for that loss, though, we’re already looking to what’s next — and there is absolutely reason to be excited there. You may unsubscribe at any time. For this, we…

title: T-Mobile is getting stingier with device discounts — will you pay more?
summary: Accounts with multiple lines won't get as many deals.
body: TL;DR T-Mobile has made changes to its device promotions. Accounts can now take advantage of one device promo on up to two service lines, down from four. Many free lines are also now ineligible for free device promos. T-Mobile’s deals are getting worse for people on family plans. The carrier has made changes to its promotions for plans with multiple lines that’ll mean some customers end up paying more for the same devices. The Mobile Report reports that two significant changes have been made to T-Mobile’s promotional deals. Starting today, any given promo deal can only be applied twice per account, regardless of how many service lines the account has. Many free lines — sometimes offered to customers as part of various promotions — are also now ineligible for device promos.

title: Pixel 11 Pro XL renders show Google going all-in with new camera bar
summary: These latest renders complete our preview of this year's non-foldable models.
body: TL;DR A new set of Pixel 11 Pro XL CAD renders gives us maybe our best look yet at how this phone is taking shape. The release follows similar imagery sets for both the Pixel 11 and Pixel 11 Pro. Like those other models, the Pixel 11 Pro XL could introduce a new camera bar design. With April now here, this summer’s upcoming smartphone launces feel closer than ever, and we’re already thinking about which models we’re going to be obsessing over the most. Easily one of the biggest highlights each year is Google’s latest crop of Pixel handsets, and if the company sticks with recent tradition, it’s likely we’ll be seeing those debut this August. So far this week, we’ve been getting an early peek at what to expect, first through the publication of some Pixel 11 CAD renders , followed by a set showing off the Pixel 11 Pro . Today we’re following this path to its next logical destination, with the release of some lovely new Pixel 11 Pro XL renders. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: Your Samsung TV is getting a massive upgrade for free with Google Cast
summary: Google Cast lands on Samsung TVs, and it’s not just for new models.
body: Credit: C. Scott Brown / Android Authority TL;DR Samsung TVs are finally getting native Google Cast, ending years of reliance on Smart View. The feature is coming to select 2023 and 2024 models via a One UI Tizen update. Rollout appears gradual, with users already spotting it on models like the S90D OLED. For a long time, Samsung TVs ran on their own Tizen-based interface instead of Android TV , which meant one frustrating limitation: no native Google Cast support. If you wanted to cast from an Android phone, you usually had to use Samsung’s Smart View, which worked best with Galaxy devices and felt like a workaround for everyone else. That’s now changing. Samsung has already confirmed that its 2026 TV lineup will include built-in Google Cast. Now, the company is bringing this feature to some older models, specifically select 2023 and 2024 TVs, through a new One UI Tizen update.

title: Samsung’s QuickStar update lets you customize your Quick Settings like never before
summary: The newest update introduces panel item resizing, landscape layout settings, and numeric slider levels in One UI 8.5.
body: TL;DR Samsung’s QuickStar Good Lock module is updating to version 10.0.04.26 for One UI 8.5 builds. Key additions include panel item resizing, numeric bar levels, and a panel style customizer. The new features are exclusive to One UI 8.5 and will not appear on older software versions. One UI 8.5 beta has now started rolling out to several older Galaxy flagships , so plenty of users can enjoy the new update. If you’re testing the new release on your Galaxy phone, you’d be glad to learn that the QuickStar Good Lock module is also receiving an update, bringing along a bunch of new features. Tech enthusiast Tarun Vats shared on X that QuickStar is being updated to v10.0.04.26. This release brings the following new features to One UI 8.5 builds:

title: This promising PC game emulator just got support for Pixel 10
summary: The Pixel 10 is a step down from its predecessor for emulation, but there's good news courtesy of GameNative.
body: Credit: Hadlee Simons / Android Authority TL;DR The GameNative app for running PC games on Android has received a hefty update. GameNative v0.9.0 now offers initial support for the Pixel 10’s GPU as well as Dex functionality. Other notable additions include download and storage manager pages, Steam Workshop support, and a battery temperature metric. Winlator and GameHub are two of the more popular ways to locally run PC games on your Android device. However, GameNative has also emerged as one of the more impressive efforts in recent months. We’ve seen a few major updates recently, but the latest version might be the biggest one yet. GameNative version 0.9.0 is now available to download as a pre-release version, and it brings a laundry list of changes and additions . Perhaps the most notable addition is initial support for the Pixel 10 ‘s PowerVR GPU. That’s a big deal, as most PC game emulators on Android primarily support the Adreno GPU in Snapdragon processors. These apps also have some support for Arm’s Mali graphics, but that’s usually where support ends.

title: I’m testing Android 17’s app bubbles, and I’ve already found a big problem
summary: App bubbles are off to a good, but imperfect, start.
body: Last year, I switched to a Pixel 10 Pro after six years with Samsung, and while I love my Pixel and have no intention of going back, there are things I miss from the Samsung experience . Pop-up windows are one of the features I miss the most, and to my surprise and delight, Google’s version — app bubbles — are available to test in Android 17 Beta 3. My happiness is immeasurable, and my day is made. At least it would be if I hadn’t already found a major annoyance with Google’s new multitasking feature.

title: Motorola’s Razr 2026 colors could prove foldables are the ultimate fashion accessory
summary: Three possible finishes, three distinct feels.
body: Motorola Razr (2025) Credit: Ryan Haines / Android Authority TL;DR The Razr 2026’s colors have been leaked, and they could include Pantone Sparkling Green, Pantone Hematite, and a softer pink option. Sparkling Green stands out with a carbon-fiber-like texture and eye-catching look. Hematite leans classic with a fabric-style finish that feels more premium. It seemed like Motorola was finished experimenting with Pantone colors, but a new Razr leak has surfaced. This time, the Razr 2025 successor seems to be moving away from the usual eco-leather back and offering a finish you’ll actually want to touch. A new report from YTECHB says the Motorola Razr 2026 will come in three main colors: Pantone Sparkling Green, Pantone Hematite, and a pink version.

title: Google creates confusion with a “new” Wear OS 6.1 update
summary: Developer documentation lists features for a "new" update that actually launched late last year.
body: Credit: Kaitlyn Cimino / Android Authority TL;DR Google updated its developer documentation with a “new” Wear OS 6.1 tag, even though the version launched in late 2025. The documentation highlights new features, including location-based time zone detection and a “graduation” process for supervised kids’ accounts, but does not acknowledge the December 2025 release. We’ve reached out to Google for clarification on the matter. Google launched Wear OS 6 back in July 2025, debuting on Samsung’s Galaxy Watch 8 and Watch 8 Classic and then making its way to Pixel Watches in October. The company then launched Wear OS 6.1 in December 2025. However, there’s some weirdness going on, as Google is now marking “Wear OS 6.1” as a new release in its developer documentation. 9to5Google mentions that Google quietly released Wear OS 6.1 based on Android 16 QPR2, but that’s incorrect, as that release was already live late last year. The report likely got confused by the “New” tag in Google’s Android Developers documentation .

title: Check your inbox: Google Fi is giving $20 credit for this week’s service outage
summary: Along with the credit, Google Fi has apologized for the recent outage.
body: Credit: Edgar Cervantes / Android Authority TL;DR Following a recent service outage, Google is reaching out to customers offering a service credit. A Reddit user has reportedly received $20 in compensation for the outage. The issue, which lasted several hours, has now been resolved. Google Fi is a relatively low-fuss mobile operator and easily among our top recommendations for alternatives to the Big 3 . While it too has its flaws , service outages aren’t typically among them. However, the MVNO recently experienced a disruption that left many users disgruntled, and now wants to apologize to those affected. Earlier this week, several Fi users received a “Your SIM card is no longer active” warning on their phones. Google also acknowledged the issue, along with the sheer scale of the outage. The exact reason wasn’t publicly revealed, but Google was able to patch the issue, although not immediately.

title: I held the OnePlus Nord 6, and it feels nothing like a 9,000mAh phone
summary: The Nord 6 is way lighter and slimmer than I expected.
body: Credit: Tushar Mehta / Android Authority TL;DR This is the OnePlus Nord 6 in real-life images ahead of its launch next week. Despite its massive 9,000mAh battery, the OnePlus Nord 6 feels light and slim. It’s powered by a Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 chip and comes with a 50MP primary camera. The Nord 6 is OnePlus’ first phone to launch globally with a humongous 9,000mAh battery . Before its launch on April 7 , OnePlus has already teased most of its specifications and what to expect from the design, but there’s definitely more to talk about. Ahead of the launch, OnePlus has sent us a unit of the Nord 6, which allows us to bring you real-life images of the phone. While OnePlus plans to launch the Nord 6 in three colors, we have it in Mist Green. The color is a tad similar to the green we once saw on phones like the OnePlus 8 or the 10T, even though the design is more in line with the recent OnePlus flagships.

title: Hot deal: Samsung Galaxy Watch ultra (2025) price drops by 53%!
summary: if you like the color white and need a spectacular smartwatch, this deal is for you!
body: Credit: C. Scott Brown / Android Authority If you own an Android phone and want the ultimate premium smartwatch, the Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra (2025) is your best bet. It’s mighty expensive, but today you can save 53% on it. It’s just $299.99 right now! Buy the Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025 for just $299.99 ($350 off)

title: New T-Mobile 5G backup internet rules are a win for some but a loss for others
summary: The switch to hourly limits is good news for streamers and gamers, but a step down for always-on users.
body: Credit: Joe Maring / Android Authority TL;DR T-Mobile is switching its Home Internet Backup plan from a 130GB data cap to 100 hours of usage. The change could benefit heavy-use bursts, such as gaming and streaming. It’s a downgrade for always-on, low-bandwidth use cases, though it’s now cheaper than it was at launch. For T-Mobile users, the Home Internet Backup plan can be a handy safety net to keep you online when your main connection goes down. The carrier is now tweaking how that backup data is measured, and whether you think the change is good or bad entirely depends on how you use data at home. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: Google questions family’s X-rated Gemini account-ban story: It doesn’t work that way
summary: Actions taken on a child's account shouldn't result in other family members being banned.
body: TL;DR A family recently claimed that their household lost their Google accounts following a teenager’s sexual Gemini encounter. Google points out that account bans don’t work in the way the allegation describes. The company also hasn’t found any evidence of recent bans fitting this pattern. Losing access to an account is one of the worst things many of us fear happening to us online, and when it’s a Google account with so much of your digital life tied to it, that impact is going to be all the more severe. We just shared with you the story about how some poor choices by one family member reportedly led to the whole household having their Google accounts banned , but now Google is pointing out a few potential holes in their story. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: Get the Google Pixel 10 at a record-low price and save $250
summary: The Google Pixel 10 has never been cheaper than this!
body: Credit: Robert Triggs / Android Authority We saw the Google Pixel 10 hit its record-low price during the Amazon Big Spring Sale. That offer is now gone, but you can still get the Google Pixel 10 for $549 from Woot.com ! Buy the Google Pixel 10 for just $549 ($250 off)

title: Google Meet is making an important change to who can download recordings
summary: You'll soon have to start disabling Google Meet downloads manually.
body: Credit: Edgar Cervantes / Android Authority TL;DR Google Meet viewers will soon be able to download or copy video recordings by default. The recording owner will need to manually restrict this setting if they want to prevent viewers from downloading or copying. This change will be made on April 30, 2026. There are plenty of reasons why you might want to download or copy a Google Meet call. Maybe you missed a part of the meeting and want to go back to see what you missed. Or maybe someone was absent, so you want to send them the recording. In a couple of weeks, you’ll be able to do just that without the need for permission from the recording owner. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: Google Photos finally arrives on Samsung TVs, but not all models
summary: Your Samsung TV finally has the Google Photos support it deserves.
body: Credit: Megan Ellis / Android Authority TL;DR Google Photos integration has rolled out to Samsung TVs. You’ll be able to access your memories through the Daily Plus row, Daily Plus app launcher, and Daily Board widget. This integration is only available on eligible 2026 Samsung TVs. Wish that you could view all of your favorite photos on the biggest screen in your home? Right before the end of 2025, Samsung announced it was working to bring Google Photos to its TVs . If you’ve been patiently waiting for this integration, then good news, Google Photos support has arrived. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: Samsung Movingstyle M7 Smart Monitor drops to its cheapest price ever
summary: Monitors don't have to stay at your desk. This one can be moved and used anywhere in the house!
body: Credit: Samsung We’re used to monitors being static; they usually sit on your desk and are rarely moved. Things are changing, though, and modern smart monitors are becoming more mobile. They are also getting more affordable, especially if you can catch a good deal on something like the Samsung Movingstyle M7 32-Inch Smart Monitor. Buy the Samsung Movingstyle M7 32-Inch Smart Monitor for $549.99 ($150 off)

title: No Pixel phone? Google’s got a backup way to update your Pixelsnap Charger
summary: Your Pixelsnap Charger needs updates too — here’s how to get them!
body: TL;DR Google introduced its Pixelsnap Charger alongside last year’s Pixel 10 series. Firmware updates allow the Charger to stay up to day with the latest Qi wireless standard. In addition to push updates through Pixel phones, Google has developed a manual path towards installing Pixelsnap Charger updates. These days, with so many of the devices in our lives actively connected to the internet, it’s easy to take for granted how effortless it’s gotten to distribute updates . When new software’s available, a company can just publish it online, and all its devices or apps can check back home and retrieve the new code. But how do we get updates out to hardware that’s not online all the time — or at all? Today we’re sharing a PSA for owners of Google’s Pixelsnap Charger , as you may not be aware of all your upgrade options here. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: No joke: Sony could make some drastic changes to the Xperia 1 VIII’s design
summary: These Sony Xperia 1 VIII renders suggest the phone won't be the Xperia you remember.
body: Credit: Super_Freak_ TL;DR Renders of what’s believed to be the Sony Xperia 1 VIII have surfaced. While the images don’t depict the final design, they do suggest a significant change could be coming. A rumor suggests that the phone will have a larger sensor, which could explain the change to the camera island. Sony’s Xperia 1 series has hardly changed over the years, with each iteration adopting the same or similar design language as the model before it. However, that may not be the case for the Xperia 1 VIII. Renders of the device have recently been circulating around, and they hint at some major changes compared to the Xperia 1 VII . Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: That cheap Lenovo tablet you were eyeing might not be so cheap anymore
summary: A range of Lenovo Android tablets have seen price hikes in the US, with some models now up to $70 more expensive.
body: Credit: Ryan Haines / Android Authority TL;DR Several models of Lenovo Android tablets in the US have quietly increased in price on the company’s official website. Price increases are mostly between $30 and $70, and Lenovo hasn’t provided an official explanation. Rising RAM costs affecting the whole tech industry may be a factor. Lenovo has carved out a nice niche for itself over the years with Android tablets that punch above their price. If you’ve ever gone looking for a solid iPad alternative without spending big money, there’s a good chance you’ve come across one of its devices. But they may not look quite so appealing on your next search, as a number of Lenovo’s tablets have quietly gone up in price in the US. As spotted by PhoneArena , several models on Lenovo’s official US store now carry higher list prices than before, with no obvious explanation from the company. The increases affect a wide range of devices, from budget options to more premium picks.

title: This smart home upgrade lets cameras share multiple streams at once without crushing your bandwidth
summary: Matter 1.5.1 also brings a big improvement for doorbell chimes.
body: Credit: Stephen Schenck / Android Authority TL;DR The Connectivity Standards Alliance has released Matter 1.5.1, which introduces several improvements for cameras and doorbells. The update makes camera streaming more efficient and brings support for multi-stream video and audio delivery. It also adds support for HEIC images, doorbell chime enhancements, and more. When the Connectivity Standards Alliance (CSA) released Matter 1.5 last year, the update brought support for cameras and video doorbells. The CSA is now building on that support with the release of Matter 1.5.1 . While it’s not a big update, it does include several quality-of-life improvements that you’ll likely find pretty handy. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: Move over Galaxy Z TriFold, this foldable is an accordion
summary: Prank or no prank, a TV that fits into your pocket sounds pretty cool.
body: Credit: Twitter/Honorglobal TL;DR HONOR has revealed the Magic VVVVVVVV foldable phone as an April Fool’s Day prank. The company says this is the first smartphone that turns into a TV. The announcement also comes as Samsung discontinues the Galaxy Z TriFold. It’s April Fool’s Day, so naturally some smartphone makers are getting in on the action. OPPO has already revealed an Android-powered 4K umbrella , and now HONOR has announced a tongue-in-cheek fictional product. But is it weird that I’m not completely opposed to this concept? HONOR announced the HONOR Magic VVVVVVVVV on social media today. Or is it the Magic VVVVVVVVVV? Either way, this puts the HUAWEI Mate XT and Galaxy Z TriFold devices to shame. Check out the official image at the top of the page.
